title: AS-REP Roasting Workflow (Linux + Windows)
description: This runbook operationalizes AS-REP Roasting Workflow (Linux + Windows) using #impacket , #rubeus , #hashcat . It is designed for repeatable, high-signal execution in authorized OSCP/CPTS-style assessments. Harvests AS-REP material from preauth-disabled users and cracks offline.

metadata.descriptionHtml:
This runbook operationalizes AS-REP Roasting Workflow (Linux + Windows) using #impacket , #rubeus , #hashcat. It is designed for repeatable, high-signal execution in authorized OSCP/CPTS-style assessments.

Harvests AS-REP material from preauth-disabled users and cracks offline.

  1. Prepare scope and access: validate target reachability and select low-noise execution order.
  2. Execute core workflow: run the command chain to produce actionable security outcomes.
  3. Validate and operationalize output: confirm results, capture artifacts, and prepare follow-on actions.
  • Tool Location/Source: primary references are in local source corpus entries linked from runbook-list.md; e.g., docs/sources-runbooks/oscp-cpts/p0/n0xturne-OSCP-Cheat-Sheet-2024/README.md:1680; docs/sources-runbooks/oscp-cpts/p0/n0xturne-OSCP-Cheat-Sheet-2024/README.md:1691.
  • Attack pre-requisite: authorized scope, operator shell access, and protocol reachability on TCP 80, TCP 443.
  • What attacker gain: validated findings (access paths, credentials, execution context, or exposure evidence) enabling faster compromise progression.
  • Detection/Defense opsec: monitor abnormal auth/enumeration bursts, enforce least privilege and hardening baselines, and alert on repeated tool-pattern telemetry.

Core Commands:

impacket-GetNPUsers -dc-ip <DC_IP> -request -outputfile hashes.asreproast <DOMAIN>/<USER>
.\Rubeus.exe asreproast /nowrap
hashcat -m 18200 hashes.asreproast /usr/share/wordlists/rockyou.txt -r /usr/share/hashcat/rules/best64.rule --force
extraInfo: primary references are in local source corpus entries linked from runbook-list.md ; e.g., docs/sources-runbooks/oscp-cpts/p0/n0xturne-OSCP-Cheat-Sheet-2024/README.md:1680; docs/sources-runbooks/oscp-cpts/p0/n0xturne-OSCP-Cheat-Sheet-2024/README.md:1691 .
prerequisites: authorized scope, operator shell access, and protocol reachability on TCP 80 , TCP 443 .

steps.name: Preparation and Target Validation
steps.description: Operator initializes the workflow and verifies prerequisites for AS-REP Roasting Workflow (Linux + Windows).
steps.tooltip: Early validation reduces noise and prevents invalid follow-on actions against unreachable or mismatched targets.

steps.name: Primary Execution
steps.description: Main command path executes against the target service or domain component.
steps.tooltip: The technique leverages service behavior, protocol assumptions, or misconfiguration to extract actionable state.

steps.name: Expansion and Verification
steps.description: Follow-up commands validate initial findings and expand result coverage.
steps.tooltip: Secondary checks reduce false positives and expose deeper privilege or access pathways.

steps.name: Consolidation for Follow-On Operations
steps.description: Final command stage consolidates output for cracking, pivoting, reporting, or escalation decisions.
steps.tooltip: Structured artifact capture enables deterministic downstream runbooks and profile ingestion.
